items.headline: U.S. Stocks End Week Lower Amid Oil Price Surge and Rate Cut Uncertainty
items.summary: U.S. stock markets concluded the trading week on a downbeat note, with major indices recording their fourth consecutive weekly loss on Friday, March 20, 2026. The prevailing market sentiment was dominated by escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which sent oil prices soaring, and growing skepticism regarding potential interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve this year. This combination fueled a broad risk-off trading environment.

items.statContext: Down 1.5% (-100.01 points) on Friday. Down 1.9% for the week.
Down 2.0% (-443.08 points) on Friday. Down 2.1% for the week.
Down 1.0% (-443.96 points) on Friday. Down 2.1% for the week.

items.headline: FedEx Tops Earnings Expectations
items.summary: FedEx reported better-than-expected earnings per share of $5.25 for its fiscal Q1 2026, surpassing estimates by $1.13. Revenue also exceeded forecasts at $24 billion, up 8.3% year-over-year. The shipping giant cited growth in its express, ground, and freight divisions, and the ability to pass on fuel surcharges.

items.headline: Micron Technology Soars on AI Demand
items.summary: Micron Technology posted a sharp surge in Q2 fiscal 2026 earnings, driven by booming demand for memory chips used in artificial intelligence and data centers. The company set new records across revenue, gross margin, EPS, and free cash flow, and its board approved a 30% increase in the quarterly dividend. Citi also raised its price target for $MU to $510 from $430.

items.headline: Planet Labs Rockets on Strong Q4 Results
items.summary: Earth observation specialist Planet Labs saw its stock rocket approximately 26% higher on March 20 after reporting breakeven adjusted EPS in Q4 alongside 41% revenue growth, both significantly beating Wall Street's expectations.

items.headline: Nike Hits 52-Week Low
items.summary: Nike's stock hit a fresh 52-week low near $52 on March 20, weighing on the consumer discretionary sector. The company is reportedly navigating a challenging market environment.

items.headline: Lululemon Shares Struggle on Downbeat Guidance
items.summary: Despite beating Q4 earnings expectations, Lululemon Athletica shares struggled for direction on Friday. The movement was influenced by downbeat guidance, increased tariff costs, and continued weakness in its Americas segment.

items.headline: Super Micro Computer Plunges
items.summary: Shares of Super Micro Computer plunged 28.2% on March 20 after U.S. authorities accused a senior executive and two affiliates of conspiring to smuggle advanced Nvidia-based servers to China.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) decided to maintain the target range for the federal funds rate at 3.50% to 3.75% during its March 18, 2026 meeting. This decision, with only one dissent in favor of a cut, comes despite an upgrade to inflation projections. The Fed is balancing a war-driven energy price shock and a weakening labor market, with the market now pricing in no rate cuts and even a small probability of a rate hike this year.

items.headline: Soaring Oil Prices Fuel Inflation Worries and Impact Markets
items.summary: Retail fuel prices are climbing nationwide due to rising crude oil costs, with the national average for regular gasoline reaching $3.925 on March 21, 2026, a 28-cent increase from the previous week. The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly disruptions to shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, is a primary driver of this surge. Elevated oil prices are intensifying inflation concerns and impacting central bank policy expectations globally.

items.headline: Producer Prices Rise as Labor Market Shows Weakness
items.summary: New Producer Price Index (PPI) data released recently showed that producer prices rose more than twice as fast as expected last month. This comes as the labor market continued to show cracks in February, with nonfarm payrolls falling by 92,000 jobs. These economic indicators suggest persistent inflationary pressures alongside a weakening job environment.

items.headline: Oracle's AI Empire Drives Strong Earnings Growth
items.summary: Oracle ($ORCL) reported that its fiscal third-quarter revenue and earnings both grew at least 20%, marking the first time the company has achieved such growth in 15 years. This performance is attributed to Oracle's growing presence in cloud infrastructure services, particularly those purpose-built for AI workloads. The company ended the quarter with a significant $553 billion in remaining purchase obligations.

items.headline: Elon Musk Found Liable in Twitter Shareholder Fraud Case
items.summary: A federal jury found Elon Musk liable on claims he defrauded Twitter shareholders by attempting to drive down the social media company's stock price to renegotiate or withdraw from his $44 billion takeover in 2022. Damages in the case will be determined at a later date.

items.headline: NTN Driveshaft Plant Closure Signals Automotive Sector Headwinds
items.summary: NTN USA Corp. announced the closure of its CVJ East Plant in Columbus, Indiana, within the next two years. Experts suggest this move, partly influenced by tariffs and declining U.S. auto sales, could be a sign of broader weakness in the automotive manufacturing sector and the local economy.
